thesis: U.S.-based manufacturer of zinc-bromine battery energy storage systems for utility-scale applications. Non-flammable aqueous chemistry offers safety advantages over lithium-ion. $305.3M DOE loan guarantee provides critical funding support. Revenue grew 632% in 2025 to $114.2M with $701.5M backlog (2.8 GWh), but gross margins remain deeply negative (-126%) during manufacturing scale-up. Path to profitability depends on achieving positive gross margins by Q4 2026.

drivers: Gross margins turn positive by Q4 2026
DOE loan funds full manufacturing expansion to 8 GWh
Zinc-bromine chemistry gains acceptance vs lithium-ion
IRA manufacturing credits provide meaningful cash contribution

risks: Lithium-ion prices drop faster than expected, eroding cost advantage
Cash burn exceeds expectations; dilutive raise required
Customer project delays push revenue to 2027
Technical issues with Z3 or Indensity products

base_case.assumptions: Revenue reaches $350M in 2026 (middle of guidance); Gross margins improve but stay negative through mid-2027; Successful DOE loan draw but slower ramp than planned; Market assigns 3x EV/Sales on 2029E revenue of $900M.

catalysts.event: DOE Loan Milestones — Critical funding trigger

key_risks: Negative gross margins — losing $1.26 for every $1 of sales
Heavy $1.76B debt load with convertible note dilution risk
Competition from established lithium-ion players (Tesla, Fluence, CATL)
